title: For People With Australian Release Quality Interlacing
display_title: For People With Australian Release Quality Interlacing
wikitext:
[]: For People With Australian Release: Quality? Interlacing?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Nov 19, 2008 10:30 pm I figured I'd buy the Australian release of 1.0 instead of waiting for it to come out here and a most likely mediocre DVD, but I'd like to know first: -How is the brightness/detail level in comparison to the Japanese release? I'm guessing it's the same, but I'd just though I'd check. God forbid the quality is lower , as the screenshots on Red Ant's site appear to be. -Is the DVD interlaced? American DVDs almost ALWAYS tend to be interlaced even if the DVD has enough space for the film to not have to be, but I don't know what the trends are for R4 releases.

[]:             Quote Post by WhiteHand » Thu Nov 20, 2008 4:26 am I'm not even sure if it's been released in Australia yet. Despite the release date being last week or similar, it's not in the shops, on any online stores and Red Ant still has it listed on preorder. Can any other Aussies confirm/deny this? Hai, I'm new, btw.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Nov 20, 2008 9:35 am WhiteHand wrote: I'm not even sure if it's been released in Australia yet. Despite the release date being last week or similar, it's not in the shops, on any online stores and Red Ant still has it listed on preorder. Can any other Aussies confirm/deny this? Hai, I'm new, btw.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat Nov 22, 2008 9:45 pm Yes, its out in shops, I've got it. Go to JB you fools. The quality is good, I certainly didn't find it any WORSE than what I've seen from the fan dub of the dvd or from screen shots. The 5.1 is awesome though.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sun Nov 23, 2008 7:58 am You didn't talk about the brightness though.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 23, 2008 2:33 pm Or the interlacing.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 23, 2008 6:09 pm WAAAAAIT A MIIINUUUUTE.... Fazmotron wrote: The 5.1 is awesome though.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Nov 23, 2008 6:51 pm Most likely the back channel.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Mon Nov 24, 2008 2:38 am NAveryW wrote: In the Japanese release it's 6.1! Which channel was removed?!

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Mon Nov 24, 2008 7:51 am Jinroh wrote: You didn't talk about the brightness though.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Nov 24, 2008 2:03 pm Video on DVDs are not interlaced. They are simply a certain dimension and aspect ratio with a fixed framerate to facilitate the DVD player to convert the stream to a signal for a TV. The signal has 2 fields per frame, each showing a portion of the frame, these fields can either be interlaced (every odd or even line of the frame) or progressive (the frame is halved). What I think NAveryW is referring to, is the artifacts added to video which facilitates players that output an interlaced signal. It makes the video look like it's interlaced when you play it on a computer or a sufficiently detailed progressive scan TV (with a progressive scan signal), but when viewed through an interlaced signal (the TV signals, NTSC in the U.S. and NTSC-j in Japan are both interlaced) it looks perfectly fine, and in some respects EVEN BETTER because the video is taking advantage of the interlacing to smooth out movement (animated or live-action, doesn't matter) when there aren't enough source frames. For example, film is 20fps, NTSC is ~29.97, at some point frames will either need to be duplicated or blended together via interlacing them (or sometimes you see simply 2 frames visually blended) in order to make up the needed frames when transfering film to NTSC media. The way you can tell if this is happening is pause and frame step through a sequence of frames that have a lot of horizontal movement, like someone moving their arm quickly or someone running. If you can see horizontal lines (every other line) from the previous frame, then the video has been processed to have these interlacing artifacts. Example taken from my Card Captor Sakura DVD: You can see that this frame consists of every other line from the previous frame and every other line from the next frame blended together to form a new frame. Most new DVDs don't have these artifacts because people have been moving away from the standard analog TV signals in favor of higher definition ones.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 24, 2008 2:25 pm The term "interlaced" is often used to refer to an image that contains two fields; that is indeed what I was referring to. Thank you for the demonstration, Ornette. EDIT: Also, that Red Ant image showing Unit 01 tripping is rather terrible. It has a ridiculously huge number of visual artifacts... I really hope those are there due to the compression of the ImageShack image.

[]:             Quote Post by WhiteHand » Tue Nov 25, 2008 12:01 am Alright, bought it last night, and watched it. The quality was fairly good. However, something I did notice was that with some shots, it almost seems like the framerate suddenly falls. Like, not slow motion, but really jerky. Like when the buildings are coming out of the geofront, it seems like every second frame has been skipped or something, so it doesn't seem very clean. I'll have to rewatch it to verify, but unfortunately one of my friends has already snaffled the DVD. And she says apparently I won't be getting it back, haha.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Nov 25, 2008 1:59 am She doesn't sound like a friend at all. She just sounds like a monster.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Tue Nov 25, 2008 2:08 am Well, I've looked at it again and it does look like it is interlaced.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Mon Dec 15, 2008 6:38 am If you want my advice have a look at the DVD from Singapore. I have found that the video quality is marginally better but you can hardly tell. As far as the Aussie copy goes it’s cheaper so who’s complaining? For the brightness levels and yes I read NAveryW's post about that, I found that if I just adjust the brightness of my TV when I'm watching the dark parts that works. Hmm wonders why that wasn’t bought up before hmmmm? Final little add In before America

[]:             Quote Post by pikadourei » Tue Dec 16, 2008 2:44 pm America
